2010.text: Over the last decade, Silicon Valley has transformed every individual with access to the Internet into a researcher. It used to be that you needed a Masters degree or a Ph.D. in a particular field, ties to highly specialized experts and tomes buried in dusty university libraries, to meet the myriad intellectual demands of work and life. Now — with a little help from Google and Wikipedia – anyone can sit down at a computer and tap into the vast knowledge base of experts who have spent weeks, years, even decades, researching a field. This transformation occurred so seamlessly that at first, we barely noticed its profound effects. However it can no longer be denied that in today’s work environment, being able to deepen our understanding of an issue has made us all the more valuable. Indeed, it is hard to imagine someone competing effectively without access to this ever-growing knowledge base. We at Palantir Technologies believe that the next seismic shift already has begun. We are being forced to answer questions reliably and expeditiously, armed not only with a researcher’s understanding of a large data set but also with an intelligence analyst’s sophisticated ability to find anomalous patterns upon which exceedingly important developments turn. However, those expectations can only be met with the right technology, technology that so far, has been lacking. At present, obtaining this level of reliable intelligence requires you to identify, recruit, and train highly qualified software engineers who are capable of writing the kind of algorithms needed to answer the complex questions you need asked and answered. Before Palantir, this was a daunting, expensive and time-consuming task.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: Palantir was built by technologists serious about protecting privacy and civil liberties. Thoughts on Palantir and the promise of analysis by our CEO, Alex Karp. Read » The Palantir White Papers. Learn more about the science behind our technology. Learn about Palantir with Operation Tradestop , an interactive analysis game leveraging Palantir Government.
2010.heading: Palantir’s Commitment to Protecting Civil Liberties

2010.text: At Palantir, we are only hiring the best and the brightest computer engineers to help us harness this awesome power to meet today’s vast analytical needs. As the CEO and founder of Palantir Technologies, I can say with pride that every engineer on the Palantir team is working with a sense of excitement and mission, convinced that we have created a product that will help analysts take quantum leaps in their knowledge base and their fields of particular expertise. Dr. Alex Karp is co-founder and Chief Executive Officer of Palantir Technologies. Prior to Palantir, Dr. Karp founded the Caedmon Group. He earned his law degree from Stanford University and his Ph.D. from the University of Frankfurt, Germany. A U.S. citizen, Dr. Karp is bilingual in Eng­lish and German, and also speaks French.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: Palantir places all the analytical power and technical prowess of the world’s best software engineers at your fingertips. Palantir shifts the paradigm away from both the etching technologies that merely let you map out what you already know and the quasi stand-alone field of artificial intelligence, that while useful for some types of analysis, has limits when it comes to making sense out of massive, complex, financial or intelligence information. We facilitate human-computer synthesis. You ask the questions and you visualize the data in ways that are useful to you and your mission. Through the user-friendly Palantir analytical platforms, the answers come forth just as if you had a team of top engineers working for hours to present you with the solutions to difficult problems. At present, the world of analysis is full of simple, linear tools that are built to go from A to B to C. Once built, these programs need massive infusions of cash and engineering to add any requirements changes. At Palantir, we’ve created a rich "conceptual environment," where each analyst can create his or her own visual intuitive space where they can absorb, understand, and interact with disparate information to come up with sophisticated findings. Much like a camera, the analyst can switch from a telephoto to a zoom lens to look at disparate bits of information, and divine their relationships. When necessary, analysts can leave their "space" and collaborate with other analysts, each sharing his or her interpretation of a data set while always maintaining the structural integrity of his or her own space. The intelligence or financial analyst can then make decisions based on the hard information that results from these real-time interactions with the data.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2011.text: Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information. We were founded in 2004 by a handful of PayPal alumni and Stanford computer scientists. Since then we’ve doubled in size every year while retaining our early-stage values: a startup culture, strong work ethic, and rigorous hiring standards. We currently offer two products: Palantir Government and Palantir Finance . Both are platforms for integrating, visualizing, and analyzing the world’s information. We support many kinds of data including structured, unstructured, relational, temporal, and geospatial. Our products are built for real analysis with a focus on security, scalability, ease of use, and collaboration. Palantir Government is broadly deployed in the intelligence, defense, and law enforcement communities, and is spreading rapidly by word-of-mouth. Palantir Finance is in use at some of the world’s leading hedge funds and financial institutions. Palantir is headquartered in sunny downtown Palo Alto, CA with offices in Tysons Corner, VA , New York and London, UK .
2011.heading: About Palantir

2012.text: A Grameen Foundation Community Knowledge worker speaks with a Ugandan farmer. A piece recently published on both the Scientific American and Fast Company’s CoExist websites highlights our most recent work with the Grameen Foundation. We participated in Hacking for Hunger, a first-of-its-kind hackathon held by the Office of Innovation & Development Alliances at USAID, which [...]
2012.heading: Grameen Foundation & Palantir: Partners for Food Security

2012.text: At the heart of what we do is a desire to connect people with the information they need to achieve extraordinary outcomes. Our solutions are revolutionizing the work being done at organizations across government, commercial and health industries. We build groundbreaking technologies , from our data fusion platforms to the extensions cooked up in our labs . Protecting civil liberties . Promoting open software . Surfacing data , not mining it. Our work is inspired by what we believe . We’re passionate about making a difference with mission-driven organizations through our philanthropic work. From the technology we build to the problems we solve and organizations we support, our work is a reflection of who we are and what we believe. We love to build things and solve hard problems, and we believe the best idea wins. Palantir is grounded in an engineering culture . From food to games to special events, life at Palantir is designed to energize and inspire. With strong mission focus , we all work together to help make the world a better place. We're looking for smart, driven people. Learn about getting hired at Palantir. Check out our student internships and other open positions . Our people are the makers and keepers of a unique culture that allows us to do serious work without taking ourselves too seriously.
2012.heading: Palantir

2012.text: Palantir was built by technologists serious about protecting privacy and civil liberties.
2012.heading: Palantir’s Commitment to Protecting Civil Liberties

2012.text: Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information. We were founded in 2004 by a handful of PayPal alumni and Stanford computer scientists. Since then we've doubled in size every year while retaining our early-stage values: a startup culture, strong work ethic and rigorous hiring standards. We currently offer a suite of software applications for integrating, visualizing and analyzing the world's information. We support many kinds of data including structured, unstructured, relational, temporal and geospatial. Our products are built for real analysis with a focus on security, scalability, ease of use and collaboration. They are broadly deployed in the intelligence, defense, law enforcement and financial communities, and are spreading rapidly by word of mouth into applications in other industries and realms of impact. Palantir is headquartered in downtown Palo Alto, CA, with offices around the world.
2012.heading: Follow us elsewhere

This year, scholarship applicants wrote essays addressing why they were pursuing a technical degree, what can be done to increase the number of women in technical fields, and what ways technology can be used to promote opportunities for women worldwide. The finalists were [...]
2013.heading: Announcing the 2012-2013 Palantir Scholarship for Women in Technology Winners!

Time’s Nation blog just published a piece describing the work of Team Rubicon in Moore, Oklahoma in the wake of the powerful tornado that created a swath of devastation on May 20, 2013. Entitled, “How Vets and [...]
2013.heading: Team Rubicon: using Palantir to improve disaster recovery

2013.text: Last month we hosted Palantir Sync: Investigate, which brought together members of the investigative, regulatory, and prosecutorial communities for a discussion of how technology and data are transforming their missions. Through a series of presentations we demonstrated how our software could provide a single, end-to-end platform for their investigations—from initial lead all the way to [...]
2013.heading: Palantir Sync brings together investigative, regulatory, and prosecutorial communities to discuss how technology can revolutionize their missions

2013.text: On November 13, Pooja Sankar, CEO of Piazza, an online Q&A platform used by students and teachers, sat down with four women at Palantir to discuss their experiences working in technology. Originally broadcasted live to students in Piazza’s network, the panelists spoke about their roles at Palantir and how they’ve navigated the ups and downs [...]
2013.heading: Panel features Palantir Women on Working in Tech

2015.text: At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world. We’re building a future where data can be leveraged to serve people, create value, and improve quality of life. Palantir interns apply their knowledge to real-world problems from day one. Learn more.
2015.heading: There is so much left to build

2015.text: Engineers build to solve problems, but at Palantir you don't have to be a computer scientist to be an engineer. You do have to speak up when things aren't right and build to fix what's broken.
2015.heading: Engineering Culture

2015.text: Where you take the next step in your career matters. While we certainly think we’re doing exciting, impactful work, we don’t want you to just take our word for it. Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.
2015.heading: Get to Know Us Better

2015.text: With a combination of perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities, we invest every day in our most important asset: our people.
2015.heading: Life At Palantir

2017.text: We believe in augmenting human intelligence, not replacing it. With good data and the right technology, people and institutions today can still solve hard problems and change the world for the better. In 2004, when we looked at the available technology, we saw products that were too rigid to handle novel problems, and custom systems that took too long to deploy and required too many services to maintain and improve. We saw automated approaches that failed against adaptive adversaries, and all-or-nothing access controls that forced organizations to make unacceptable trade-offs between collaborating and securing sensitive data from misuse. We saw a need for a different kind of technology, and we knew it would take a different kind of company to build it. That’s why we founded Palantir.
2017.heading: Why We’re Here

2017.text: Organizations around the world are using Palantir to help them do their most important work. With Palantir, investigators are uncovering human trafficking rings, finding exploited children, and unraveling complex financial crimes. Humanitarian response organizations are directing resources more effectively to communities affected by natural disasters. Prosecutors are building stronger cases against insider traders. Public health officials are tracking and containing the spread of deadly diseases. Information security specialists are defending against the theft of intellectual property, while oversight authorities are auditing user activity to protect sensitive data from misuse or abuse. And this is just the beginning. We are working to build a future in which public institutions, commercial enterprises, and non-profit organizations can use data to function as they were designed—to fulfill the mandates with which they’ve been entrusted, to deliver value to customers, and to distribute aid to those most in need.
2017.heading: Where We’re Going

2017.text: We are engineers on a mission. We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well. Some institutions have the resources to pay for our products, and some don’t. Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms the way they use data in pursuit of their goals.
2017.heading: We go where we’re needed most

2017.text: We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line. To achieve this, we build platforms for integrating, managing, and securing data on top of which we layer applications for fully interactive human-driven, machine-assisted analysis.
2017.heading: We make products for human-driven analysis of real-world data

2017.text: Analytic technology, especially in the hands of powerful institutions that hold large volumes of data, can pose serious risks to privacy and civil liberties. That's why we build privacy-protective capabilities into our products, help customers understand how to use them responsibly, and work with advocacy groups and the policy community on how technology can be used to protect privacy interests today and in the future. We have always been, and continue to be, committed to helping organizations get value out of their data while protecting sensitive information from misuse and abuse.
2017.heading: We design technology to help institutions protect liberty

Our platforms are used throughout the public, private, and non-profit sectors to help organizations quickly implement solutions to the hardest problems they face.
UK Vaccine rollout success built on NHS determination and military precision

No matter where you're based, or which team you work on, you’ll be part of a group of people working together to build solutions to mission-critical problems and a company that values the very best ideas.
We build software that empowers organizations to effectively integrate their data, decisions, and operations.
2021.heading: Palantir

2022.text: With good data and the right technology, people and institutions today can still solve hard problems and change the world for the better.

We are working to build a future in which public institutions, commercial enterprises, and non-profit organizations can use data to function as they were designed—to fulfill the mandates with which they’ve been entrusted, to deliver value to customers, and to distribute aid to those most in need.

Humanitarian response organizations are directing resources more effectively to communities affected by natural disasters. Prosecutors are building stronger cases against insider traders. Public health officials are tracking and containing the spread of deadly diseases. Information security specialists are defending against the theft of intellectual property, while oversight authorities are auditing user activity to protect sensitive data from misuse or abuse. And this is just the beginning.
Explore Palantir careers. There is so much left to build.
2022.heading: Careers | Palantir

2022.text: We saw a need for a different kind of technology, and we knew it would take a different kind of company to build it. That’s why we founded Palantir.

2023.text: Where you take the next step in your career matters. Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.

2024.text: If you want to empower the world's most important institutions, you belong here.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and within commercial and governmental institutions.

Palantir technology powers mission-critical, real-world decisions across industries, helping operators to prevent wildfires, distribute vaccines, staff nurses, improve road safety, and more. Build the future with us.

2025.text: Palantirians deliver mission-critical outcomes for the West's most important institutions.
The U.S. Army now has in hand a few Tactical Intelligence Targeting Access Nodes, state-of-the-art trucks that promise to streamline the battlefield process of spotting, tracking and blasting.

2026.text: We built Palantir to ensure the future of the West, not to tinker at the margins. On the factory floor. In the operating room. Across the battlefield. We build with consequence.
Tech culture has fallen into shallow consumerism, lacking a greater purpose. Too few in Silicon Valley have asked what ought to be built — and why.
Sleek moving text that says "The world's hardest problems need the worlds smartest people."
2026.heading: Worlds Smartest People Gif

2026.text: We built Palantir to ensure the future of the West, not to tinker at the margins.
Foundational tools that unlock defense innovation and accelerate operational impact at scale.

2012.text: Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information. We were founded in 2004 by a handful of PayPal alumni and Stanford computer scientists. Since then we've doubled in size every year while retaining our early-stage values: a startup culture, strong work ethic and rigorous hiring standards. We currently offer a suite of software applications for integrating, visualizing and analyzing the world's information. We support many kinds of data including structured, unstructured, relational, temporal and geospatial. Our products are built for real analysis with a focus on security, scalability, ease of use and collaboration. They are broadly deployed in the intelligence, defense, law enforcement and financial communities, and are spreading rapidly by word of mouth into applications in other industries and realms of impact. Palantir is headquartered in downtown Palo Alto, CA, with offices around the world.
2012.heading: Follow us elsewhere

2013.text: Congratulations to the winners of this year’s Palantir Scholarship for Women in Technology! This year, scholarship applicants wrote essays addressing why they were pursuing a technical degree, what can be done to increase the number of women in technical fields, and what ways technology can be used to promote opportunities for women worldwide. 2013.text: Team Rubicon’s William Gauntner uses Palantir Mobile client to provide immediate data to relief agencies in tornado-ravaged Oklahoma. Time’s Nation blog just published a piece describing the work of Team Rubicon in Moore, Oklahoma in the wake of the powerful tornado that created a swath of devastation on May 20, 2013. Entitled, “How Vets and [...]
2013.heading: Team Rubicon: using Palantir to improve disaster recovery

2013.text: On November 13, Pooja Sankar, CEO of Piazza, an online Q&A platform used by students and teachers, sat down with four women at Palantir to discuss their experiences working in technology. Originally broadcasted live to students in Piazza’s network, the panelists spoke about their roles at Palantir and how they’ve navigated the ups and downs [...]
2013.heading: Panel features Palantir Women on Working in Tech

2013.text: Lauren Chaparro and I were honored to be among the speakers at Strata RX 2012, O’Reilly’s conference on the use of big data in health care/medical field. Our talk was called “Doing Big Data All By Yourself: Interactive Data Driven Decision Making by Non-Programmers“. I gave the first half of the talk, delving into the [...]
2013.heading: Palantir at StrataRX 2012: Doing Big Data By Yourself

2013.text: Last month we hosted Palantir Sync: Investigate, which brought together members of the investigative, regulatory, and prosecutorial communities for a discussion of how technology and data are transforming their missions. Through a series of presentations we demonstrated how our software could provide a single, end-to-end platform for their investigations—from initial lead all the way to [...]
2013.heading: Palantir Sync brings together investigative, regulatory, and prosecutorial communities to discuss how technology can revolutionize their missions

2015.text: At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world. We’re building a future where data can be leveraged to serve people, create value, and improve quality of life. Palantir interns apply their knowledge to real-world problems from day one. Learn more.
2015.heading: There is so much left to build

2015.text: Engineers build to solve problems, but at Palantir you don't have to be a computer scientist to be an engineer. You do have to speak up when things aren't right and build to fix what's broken.
2015.heading: Engineering Culture

2015.text: Where you take the next step in your career matters. While we certainly think we’re doing exciting, impactful work, we don’t want you to just take our word for it. Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.
2015.heading: Get to Know Us Better

2015.text: With a combination of perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities, we invest every day in our most important asset: our people.
2015.heading: Life At Palantir

2017.text: Our customers have data and a deep understanding of the problems they face. We have proven products and an engineering mindset. We send our engineers into the field to work directly with our customers—deploying our products, integrating their data, optimizing their workflows, and producing operational results in weeks, not years. By establishing a true partnership, we help customers get the most out of our products and engineering expertise. See the challenges we help our customers face
2017.heading: We meet the problems where they live

2017.text: Organizations around the world are using Palantir to help them do their most important work. With Palantir, investigators are uncovering human trafficking rings, finding exploited children, and unraveling complex financial crimes. Humanitarian response organizations are directing resources more effectively to communities affected by natural disasters. Prosecutors are building stronger cases against insider traders. Public health officials are tracking and containing the spread of deadly diseases. Information security specialists are defending against the theft of intellectual property, while oversight authorities are auditing user activity to protect sensitive data from misuse or abuse. And this is just the beginning. We are working to build a future in which public institutions, commercial enterprises, and non-profit organizations can use data to function as they were designed—to fulfill the mandates with which they’ve been entrusted, to deliver value to customers, and to distribute aid to those most in need.
2017.heading: Where We’re Going

2017.text: We believe in augmenting human intelligence, not replacing it. With good data and the right technology, people and institutions today can still solve hard problems and change the world for the better. In 2004, when we looked at the available technology, we saw products that were too rigid to handle novel problems, and custom systems that took too long to deploy and required too many services to maintain and improve. We saw automated approaches that failed against adaptive adversaries, and all-or-nothing access controls that forced organizations to make unacceptable trade-offs between collaborating and securing sensitive data from misuse. We saw a need for a different kind of technology, and we knew it would take a different kind of company to build it. That’s why we founded Palantir.
2017.heading: Why We’re Here

2017.text: Analytic technology, especially in the hands of powerful institutions that hold large volumes of data, can pose serious risks to privacy and civil liberties. That's why we build privacy-protective capabilities into our products, help customers understand how to use them responsibly, and work with advocacy groups and the policy community on how technology can be used to protect privacy interests today and in the future. We have always been, and continue to be, committed to helping organizations get value out of their data while protecting sensitive information from misuse and abuse.
2017.heading: We design technology to help institutions protect liberty

2017.text: We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line. To achieve this, we build platforms for integrating, managing, and securing data on top of which we layer applications for fully interactive human-driven, machine-assisted analysis.
2017.heading: We make products for human-driven analysis of real-world data

2018.text: At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world. We’re building a future where data can be leveraged to serve people, create value, and improve quality of life.
2018.heading: There is so much left to build

2021.text: UK Vaccine rollout success built on NHS determination and military precision
Grupo Globo leverages Palantir Foundry to provide a clear picture of its advertising landscape across broadcast, cable, and digital.
Since 2018, Palantir has partnered with Grupo Globo to help the media giant harness data across its multi-channel products to achieve a digital transformation.

This partnership really created an environment conducive to the type of digital transformation we were aiming for. Being able to change the teams’ mindset around decisions and how decisions should be made using data was one of the biggest gains we had with the partnership. We managed to accelerate the cultural transformation at Globo by partnering with Palantir.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world.

2022.text: If you want to stare into the face of important problems and have the freedom to solve them, we want to work with you. We have some resources to help you navigate the hiring process.
There’s a role here for you—whether you're a new grad or have years of experience.
With Palantir, investigators are uncovering human trafficking rings, finding exploited children, and unraveling complex financial crimes.

We send our engineers into the field to work directly with our customers—deploying our products, integrating their data, optimizing their workflows, and producing operational results in weeks, not years. By establishing a true partnership, we help customers get the most out of our products and engineering expertise.
Explore Palantir careers. There is so much left to build.
2022.heading: Careers | Palantir

2023.text: Where you take the next step in your career matters. Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.
At Palantir, we believe that with good data and the right software, institutions can solve hard problems and change the world for the better.
2023.heading: About Palantir

2024.text: Palantir technology powers mission-critical, real-world decisions across industries, helping operators to prevent wildfires, distribute vaccines, staff nurses, improve road safety, and more. Build the future with us.
If you want to empower the world's most important institutions, you belong here.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and within commercial and governmental institutions.

2025.text: Palantirians deliver mission-critical outcomes for the West's most important institutions.
The U.S. Army now has in hand a few Tactical Intelligence Targeting Access Nodes, state-of-the-art trucks that promise to streamline the battlefield process of spotting, tracking and blasting.

2026.text: You are not evaluated by distant executives — you are judged by outcomes. There's no oversight from anyone who isn't directly in the problem with you. Your work will speak for itself.
2026.heading: Independents

2026.text: We attract people who are intellectually diverse and contrarian at heart. Palantirians have strong convictions and open minds. Whether you have PhD or GED, deep thinking matters most.
2026.heading: Free Thinkers

2026.text: Palantirians make decisions quickly. We optimize for impact, not consensus. There is no bureaucratic distance between identifying a problem and fixing it.
2026.heading: Fast-movers

2026.text: Palantirians take initiative. There's no top-down instruction holding you back. This creates unusual freedom: when you see a solution, you own it.
2026.heading: Catalysts

2026.text: Sleek moving text that says "The world's hardest problems need the worlds smartest people."
2026.heading: Worlds Smartest People Gif

2010.text: You ask the questions and you visualize the data in ways that are useful to you and your mission.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: The intelligence or financial analyst can then make decisions based on the hard information that results from these real-time interactions with the data.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: Before Palantir, this was a daunting, expensive and time-consuming task.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: At present, obtaining this level of reliable intelligence requires you to identify, recruit, and train highly qualified software engineers who are capable of writing the kind of algorithms needed to answer the complex questions you need asked and answered.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir

2010.text: Through the user-friendly Palantir analytical platforms, the answers come forth just as if you had a team of top engineers working for hours to present you with the solutions to difficult problems.
2010.heading: Perspectives on Palantir
